span.p:
Use auxiliary data structures
(hash maps, stacks, linked lists)

(array indices, pointers)

span.p:
Combine complementary structures
(hash map for lookup + list for order)

span.p: Design Pattern Trade-offs

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Min Stack
push, pop, top, getMin in O(1)
Stack + parallel min tracking stack

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
LRU Cache
get, put in O(1), evict least used
HashMap + Doubly Linked List

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
LFU Cache
get, put in O(1), evict least freq
HashMap + frequency linked list buckets

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
HashMap
put, get, del in O(1) avg
Array + linked list chaining / open addr

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Browser History
visit, back, forward in O(1)
Two stacks / doubly linked list
